shared_properties: Six-frame looping projectile animation with a bright center and trailing motion.
Travel readability inside the authored spell lane.
Alpha blended edge treatment that remains visible on dark background zones.
differences: Frame size expands from 40x40 px to 50x50 px to support a wider droplet shell.
Tail changes into a ribbon-like water wake with rounded edges.
Palette shifts to bright blue and pale foam highlights.

reference_images.label: reflect_academy_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Academy hall color rhythm, duel staging, card layout balance, and warm-versus-cool lighting anchor.

reference_images.label: reflect_academy_player
reference_images.purpose: Player silhouette, costume layering, hand pose, and magical focus posture anchor.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Use SpriteSheetBundle for the water projectile and share the same gameplay component stack as the fire projectile.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load obstacles/projectile_water_fly_6f_12fps.png through AssetServer and register the atlas under projectile_water_fly_6f_12fps.
runtime_notes: Water projectile can reuse the projectile movement system with a slower speed value.
Frame pivot matches the fire projectile center so color swaps do not shift the hit point.
Blue highlights must remain visible on the navy portions of the hall backdrop.

context.usage: Water projectile used for enemy water volleys and reflected water conversions that travel at 220 px per second.

shape.silhouette: Rounded droplet orb with a soft wake and tiny spray flecks.

specs.lighting: Pale foam highlight sits on the top-left edge and deep blue shading weights the rear arc.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 300x50 px with six 50x50 frames.
Droplet outline remains closed and readable with no broken edge pixels.
Wake ribbon stays inside a 14 px trail depth so collisions still read from the leading orb body.
